narration: A power of two has exactly one 1 bit in binary.
callout: Each example has one lonely on-switch.

narration: Subtracting 1 flips that one 1 bit down and turns the smaller bits on.
decision: That is why n & (n - 1) becomes zero for powers of two.

predictionPrompt.prompt: What special binary feature does every power of two have?

choices.label: Exactly one 1 bit
choices.feedback: Correct. That one-bit shape is the key clue.

choices.label: Exactly two 1 bits
choices.feedback: Not this one. Two 1 bits means it is not a power of two.

narration: If more than one 1 bit exists, the AND result will not become zero.
callout: That is why 10 is not a power of two.
